title: Learning to Learn
objective: Students will explore learning to learn concepts including Choosing a Strategy, Reflecting After Learning.

steps.content: Let's discover the amazing world of Choosing a Strategy!
Before starting a study task, choose a deliberate strategy; after finishing, evaluate honestly whether that strategy actually helped
Let's practice together! Before starting a task, name the strategy they plan to use and explain why — e.g. 'I'll make a mind map first because there's a lot to organise'
Does you ever talk about *how* they're going to study or practise something — and afterwards think about whether that approach worked?

Amazing work! Let's review what we discovered about Choosing a Strategy. Before starting a task, name the strategy they plan to use and explain why — e.g. 'I'll make a mind map first because there's a lot to organise' After finishing, honestly evaluate whether their chosen strategy helped or whether a different one would have been better
Welcome to an exciting lesson about Reflecting After Learning!
After completing a piece of learning, reflect on the process: what helped most, what was confusing, and what would you do differently next time?
Let's practice together! Kolb reflective learning cycle
After you finishes a project or topic at school, do they reflect on how the learning went — not just whether the result was good, but what worked and what didn't?
Great job! Today we learned about Reflecting After Learning. Kolb reflective learning cycle Hattie & Timperley feedback model
